title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Following his confrontation with the Red Hand Party member, Anthony retreats to his room to read the memoirs of the late Count. While ordering dinner, he notices that the waiter, an Italian man named Gisippo, appears intensely interested in the manuscripts on his table. Finding the handwriting difficult to read, Anthony finishes the fourth chapter and prepares for sleep. He locks his letters and the memoir's packaging inside a suitcase, places a chair and a water bottle against his door for defense, and hides the memoir under his pillow.

Four hours later, Anthony is awakened by a shadow near his window. Gisippo enters through the window and attacks Anthony with a long knife. During the struggle, Gisippo chokes Anthony, but the knife falls to the ground. Anthony attempts to block the door, but realizes the intruder entered through the window; Gisippo subsequently escapes by jumping to a neighboring balcony. Although Anthony manages to retrieve the memoir from under his pillow, he discovers that Gisippo has stolen the letters from his suitcase.

Meanwhile, Virginia Rue returns to her residence on Pound Street. Her butler, Chifley, informs her that a foreign man is in the study claiming to have important information regarding her late husband, Major Timothy Rue. Virginia meets the man, described as a tall foreigner of flexible build, in the study. After a tense silence, the man presents Virginia with a letter, which she begins to examine.
